texto: The sky was overcast with thick, grey clouds drifting in the direction of Idasa. That meant rain. It would come, as long as the clouds drifted in that direction. Lightening flashes momentarily parted the clouds...Shango, the god of lightening and thunder, was registering his anger as this strange talk of a new God is taking hold of simple folk who were once unquestioning votaries of his order. The new malady must be nipped in the bud.
explicacao: Orientação direta de T.M. Aluko sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “The sky was overcast with thick, grey clouds drifting in the direction of Idasa. That meant rain.…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, africa, african-literature, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e african-literature.
